[PUBLISHED DATE]: Unknown Date
[TITLE]: Eve The “Bare Bear” Makes Stunning Transformation Seven Years After Being Found In Dumpster | Bored Panda
[METADATA/INTRO]: Eve The “Bare Bear” Makes Stunning Transformation Seven Years After Being Found In Dumpster Interview With Expert(Article updated on Dec. 6 with comments from Sue Tygielski, senior director at Black Beauty Ranch.)...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: - The animal was found hairless and malnourished in a dumpster due to severe mange. 
 - Unable to survive in the wild, Eve found a permanent home at Black Beauty Ranch in Texas. 
 Mange is a disease caused by parasitic mites that affect the skin. It’s characterized by hair loss, severe itching, and the formation of scabs and lesions. Additionally, it can cause widespread secondary skin infections, extreme weight loss, blindness, and death. 
 It can impact wildlife and pets and is typically contracted by contact with another infested animal, or a contaminated environment, as per theAnimal Humane Society. 
 Eve was taken to the Fund for Animals Wildlife Center in Ramona, California, where she received treatment for her mange and a healthy diet to help her recover.

[PUBLISHED DATE]: Unknown Date
[TITLE]: Eve The “Bare Bear” Makes Stunning Transformation Seven Years After Being Found In Dumpster - AOL
[METADATA/INTRO]: Eve The “Bare Bear” Makes Stunning Transformation Seven Years After Being Found In Dumpster Eve, a black bear known as the “Bare Bear,” has grown back her thick fur coat seven years after being found completely hairless in a dumpster....
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: It can impact wildlife and pets and is typically contracted by contact with another infested animal, or a contaminated environment, as per theAnimal Humane Society. 
 A bald “bare bear” has made a stunning transformation after moving to a Texas sanctuary 
 Eve was taken to the Fund for Animals Wildlife Center in Ramona, California, where she received treatment for her mange and a healthy diet to help her recover. 
 At the Center, the bear was given regular medicated baths to make her fur grow back. Eventually, small patches began to appear. 
 By July 2018, six months after the rescue, the skin disease was mostly gone, but the animal still dealt with a skin infection that required treatment.

[PUBLISHED DATE]: 2024-09-29
[TITLE]: Phoenix Zoo mourns loss of southern white rhinoceros
[METADATA/INTRO]: Phoenix Zoo mourns loss of southern white rhinoceros PHOENIX (AZFamily)—The Phoenix Zoo is mourning the loss of a beloved animal. The zoo shared that its southern white rhinoceros, Howard, was euthanized after becoming unresponsive to treatment for a protracted and...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: Howard was born at the White Oak Conservation Center in Florida in 1997 and has been at the Phoenix Zoo since 2020, where he quickly became a favorite among staff and guests. 
 A female southern white rhinoceros named LouLou is the last of the species at the Phoenix Zoo.
